articles.title: FM Sitharaman discusses ways to boost growth with Bengal CM
articles.description: The meeting, coming just over a month after the BJP stormed to power in West Bengal for the first time, also dwelt on improving the credit availability across various sectors in the state and bolstering the implementation of central government schemes there, the finance ministry said in a statement.

articles.title: Gra sim approves Rs 3,000 cr expansion of Harihar Lyocell Plant
articles.description: Grasim Industries will invest ₹3,094 crore to expand its Lyocell plant in Harihar, Karnataka. This expansion will significantly boost its advanced fibre capabilities. The project will add 110,000 tonnes capacity, making Grasim a global leader in sustainable man-made cellulosic fibres. The plant's first phase is set for commissioning by mid-2027, with the second phase to follow in 2028 and 2030.

articles.title: PCMC plans to spend Rs 56 lakh to deploy 50 volunteers for monsoon emergency response for five months
articles.description: PCMC plans to spend Rs 56 lakh to deploy 50 volunteers for monsoon emergency response for five months

articles.title: AAL, UAL, DAL, LUV In Focus - US Airlines' Fuel Costs Soared 78% In April, Transport Department Says
articles.description: Rising energy prices driven by Middle East tensions pushed U.S. airlines’ April 2026 fuel costs sharply higher, even as carriers used less fuel overall.

articles.title: With monsoon yet to arrive, Upper Lake level drops 1.4 feet in 34 days
articles.description: With monsoon yet to arrive, Upper Lake level drops 1.4 feet in 34 days

articles.title: Banks likely to go all out to attract diaspora investment
articles.description: RBI's decision to bear the hedging cost on FCNR-B deposits and exempt banks from maintaining the cash reserve ratio and statutory liquidity ratio against those deposits would save around 300 basis points of operational cost for banks, senior bankers said. "I have told our team to raise FCNR-B rates by 150 basis points to start with. We can increase rates up to 200 basis points ," Canara Bank managing director Brajesh Kumar Singh told ET.

articles.title: HC says toll collection against construction services provided to NHAI taxable
articles.description: The Rajasthan High Court has ruled that toll collection rights are taxable. These rights are considered payment for highway construction services provided to the National Highways Authority of India. The court rejected arguments that toll collection is exempt from GST. This decision could impact public-private infrastructure projects. Tax authorities can now demand GST, interest, and penalties.

articles.title: Stocks to Watch Today: NLC India, IRB Infrastructure, Rail Vikas Nigam, JNK India, Bharti Airtel, Vodafone Idea, NRB Bearings in focus on 09 June
articles.description: Stocks to Watch, 09 June: Stocks like NLC India, IRB Infrastructure Developers, Panacea Biotec, Grasim Industries, HCL Technologies, Rail Vikas Nigam, JNK India, Motilal Oswal Financial Services, and NRB Bearings will be in focus on June 9.

articles.title: Fitch sees global oil markets returning to oversupply after Hormuz reopens
articles.description: Global oil markets are set to return to oversupply soon. Fitch Ratings reports the Strait of Hormuz closure is a temporary supply shock. Prices are expected to fall sharply once traffic resumes. The market anticipates a surplus from September. This is driven by West Asian production recovery, strong non-OPEC growth, and potential OPEC output increases.

articles.title: Vegas Group Buys over 10 acres of land in New Delhi for mixed-use project
articles.description: Vegas Group has acquired a significant land parcel in Dwarka, New Delhi. The joint venture plans to develop a large mixed-use project. This development will include a mall, offices, hotels, and serviced apartments. The project is expected to boost the region's growth and meet existing demand for commercial spaces. Investment in this venture is projected to be substantial.

articles.title: Dead insect in biriyani: Court orders restaurant to provide 10 free biriyanis and Rs 10,000 compensation
articles.description: A Puducherry restaurant was held liable for serving contaminated food after a dead insect was discovered in a biriyani plate. The consumer commission ordered the eatery to pay Rs 10,000 compensation and Rs 3,000 for litigation costs. Additionally, the restaurant must provide the customer with 10 free plates of biriyani, served over five weeks.

articles.title: MNRE forms panel to review ALMM-II exemption requests for solar projects
articles.description: Committee comprising officials from SECI, IREDA and NISE will examine requests for commissioning extensions beyond May 31, 2026, under the domestic solar cell mandate

articles.title: FMCG Distributors Warn Of Nationwide Protest, Seek Higher Margins Amid Rising Costs
articles.description: The AICPDF says distributor margins of 3.5-5% are no longer viable; sets July 30 deadline for FMCG companies to address concerns or face possible collective action in August.

articles.description: Murukku makers in Manapparai struggle with rising costs and labor shortages due to the war in West Asia, impacting production.

articles.title: Amazon, Corning sign multi-billion-dollar deal to boost fiber optics manufacturing in US
articles.description: June 8 : Amazon said on Monday it has signed a multi-billion-dollar deal with specialty glass maker Corning aimed at boosting U.S. production of optical fiber and connectivity products that are used in data centers. Amazon, however, did not disclose any further financial details on the partnership.Sha

articles.title: LIC Housing Finance asked to pay Rs 10 lakh after losing borrower’s original property papers
articles.description: LIC Housing Finance must pay Rs 10 lakh compensation for losing a borrower's original property documents after loan repayment. The consumer commission upheld the order, finding the company guilty of deficiency in service. Despite the borrower's repeated requests, the lender failed to return the crucial title deeds, leading to the significant penalty.

articles.title: Haleon bets on India with Rs 2,000-crore manufacturing facility
articles.description: In an exciting development for Indian manufacturing, Haleon, previously known as GSK Consumer Healthcare, has announced a hefty investment of Rs 2,000 crore to launch its first production unit in Pithampur, Madhya Pradesh.

articles.title: BOB Apprentice recruitment 2026: Registration ends today for 5,000 graduate posts; direct link to apply here
articles.description: Bank of Baroda's online application for 5,000 apprentice roles closes today, June 8. Graduates can apply for a one-year training program with a Rs 15,000 monthly stipend. Eligibility requires a graduation degree and registration on NAPS or NATS. Selection involves an online exam, document verification, and a local language test. Don't miss this chance for practical banking experience.

articles.title: Auto stocks decline as FADA reports month-on-month drop in May vehicle retail sales
articles.description: Auto stocks declined on Monday after the Federation of Automobile Dealers Associations (FADA) reported a month-on-month decline in vehicle retail sales for May 2026.

articles.description: H G Infra Engineering rallied 3.82% to Rs 568.05 after the company received the provisional completion certificate from Adani Road Transport for Ganga Expressway project in Uttar Pradesh (UP).

articles.description: The Berkshire Hathaway deal “opens up other options that we would not be able to do on our own”, Tokio Marine CEO Masahiro Koike said.

articles.title: Stock markets fall and oil jumps as Middle East conflict intensifies and AI boom falters – business live
articles.description: Rolling coverage of the latest economic and financial news Major chipmakers led the slump on South Korea’s stock market today. Samsung Electronics are down 9.2%, and SK Hynix has dropped by 6.4%, helping to pull the KOSPI index down by over 8%. That slump tripping circuit breakers on the Seoul stock market. Circuit breakers were activated at 0003 GMT, halting trading for 20 minutes for the first time in three months. It was the third time they were triggered this year, and the ninth in history. Continue reading...

articles.title: Sigma Advanced Systems bags ₹208 crore export order for artillery shells
articles.description: Defence manufacturer will supply 40,000 artillery shell bodies to a North American customer over the next six months, marking its expansion into ammunition component manufacturing

articles.title: Bonus issue alert! This smallcap company announced a 2:5 bonus issue. Do you own?
articles.description: Hardwyn India has announced a 2:5 bonus issue, subject to shareholder approval at an EGM on July 3. The company also plans to increase its authorised share capital to Rs 70 crore. While the record date is yet to be announced, analysts remain positive on the stock's near-term technical outlook.

articles.title: Motivational quote of the day by Narayana Murthy: "Progress is often equal to..."
articles.description: Infosys co-founder Narayana Murthy emphasizes that true progress stems from mindset, not just intellect. He explains that while the mind is our raw brainpower, the mindset acts as the operating system, filtering beliefs and expectations. Upgrading this internal software, by auditing personal rules, treating failure as data, and associating with positive influences, is crucial for overcoming obstacles and achieving breakthroughs.

articles.title: Rajesh Exports hits lower circuit for third straight session post Sebi probe
articles.description: The stock was locked at its lower circuit limit of Rs 94.50, down Rs 4.95 or 4.98 percent.

articles.title: Sterlite Tech shares slide 5% after rallying 56% in one month. Here's why
articles.description: Sterlite Technologies shares hit the lower circuit on Monday as a sharp global selloff in AI and technology stocks dented investor sentiment. The decline comes after the stock's stellar 474% rally in 2026 and a 56% gain over the past month, with shares locked at Rs 588.30 on the NSE amid profit-booking and weakening AI optimism.

articles.title: ‘I don’t hire Indians’: Owner of Indian restaurant in the US explains hiring policy
articles.description: The Indian owner of an Indian restaurant in Texas, United States, has explained why she does not hire Indians at her business.

The pattern reflects sustained bullish sentiment throughout the session, with buyers maintaining control from open to close, indicating potential continuation of upward momentum in the near term.

articles.title: Actor Sukanya wins defamation case against Sun TV after 30 years over Veerappan interview, Madras HC asks TV to pay Rs 10 lakh damage
articles.description: Actor Sukanya has won a 30-year defamation case after the Madras High Court ordered Sun TV to pay Rs 10 lakh for airing Veerappan’s scandalous claims without verification.

articles.title: ECB to hike rates as Mideast war pushes up inflation
articles.description: The European Central Bank is poised to raise interest rates for the first time in two and a half years, driven by escalating inflation fueled by the Iran war's energy shock. Despite concerns about slowing growth, the ECB aims to reassure markets by tackling rising consumer prices, which have surpassed its target.

articles.title: Gas prices today June 8: Check daily average rates in California, Texas, Florida, New York, Washington, Hawaii and more
articles.description: US gasoline prices are seeing a welcome dip, with the national average now at $4.164 per gallon, down nearly 20 cents in a week. While states like California and Washington grapple with high prices, Texas and Oklahoma offer significant relief to drivers. This trend provides a much-needed break as summer travel season kicks off.

articles.title: Nifty, Sensex likely to see gap-down open amid global tech sell-off, GIFT Nifty down 1%
articles.description: Indian markets may open muted as GIFT Nifty drops over 1%, reflecting global sell-off and risk-off sentiment.

articles.title: CMR Green Technologies IPO allotment today: Check status online as GMP signals 34% listing premium
articles.description: CMR Green Technologies IPO investors are set to receive allotment updates on June 8 after the Rs 631 crore issue witnessed overwhelming demand, with subscriptions reaching 127 times overall. Strong interest from institutional and non-institutional investors has heightened anticipation ahead of the company's market debut later this week.
